dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4716 IS: SNAP Fraud Reporting Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Scott of Florida introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry

form.official-title: To amend the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 to require States to provide data on fraud in the supplemental nutrition assistance program, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the SNAP Fraud Reporting Act of 2026.
Section 11 of the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 (7 U.S.C. 2020) is amended by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this subsection, each State shall submit to the Secretary data on fraud that occurred in the supplemental nutrition assistance program for the 5 most recent fiscal years for which such data are available.
Data submitted under paragraph (1) shall include, as applicable—

subparagraph.text: the number of supplemental nutrition assistance program fraud cases open for investigation;

the total number of supplemental nutrition assistance program fraud cases identified, including substantiated fraud, such as card skimming and card cloning, and the total dollar amount of those fraud cases;
the number and type of enforcement actions taken against fraud in the supplemental nutrition assistance program;
the amount of the recoveries in the cases described in subparagraph (C);
for each fiscal year that begins after the date of enactment of this subsection, data on—

clause.text: the number of individuals disqualified from the supplemental nutrition assistance program for fraud on the basis that the individual was receiving the benefits of a deceased person or using the identity of a deceased person who received benefits; and
the dollar amount of those benefits; and

subparagraph.text: for each fiscal year that begins after the date of enactment of this subsection, data on—

clause.text: the number of individuals disqualified from the supplemental nutrition assistance program for fraud on the basis that the individual submitted no social security number or used a falsified, recycled, stolen, or purchased social security number to be eligible for benefits; and
the dollar amount of the benefits received by those individuals.

paragraph.text: Not later than 60 days after October 1, 2028, and not later than 60 days after each October 1 thereafter, each State shall submit to the Secretary the data described in paragraph (2) for the most recently concluded fiscal year.

If a State does not provide the data required under this subsection by the applicable date, the Secretary shall withhold funds to that State under section 16 until such data is received from the State.

Not later than 180 days after receiving data from each State under paragraph (1) and annually under paragraph (3), the Secretary shall submit to Congress, and make publicly available, a report that contains the findings of the Secretary based on that data.
